headline: The Walt Disney Company Sets Leadership Team for Expanded Disney Entertainment Segment
summary: BURBANK, Calif., March 16, 2026--Dana Walden, incoming president and chief creative officer of The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS), today announced the new leadership structure for Disney Entertainment, bringing together the company’s streaming, film, and television businesses along with its growing games and digital entertainment division. Consumers today want to engage with Disney’s storytelling and characters in a multitude of ways – whether on Disney+, in theaters, or through the digital gam

headline: Oscars? Disney Doesn't Need No Stinkin' Oscar.
summary: The iconic studio collected just one of the 22 Oscars handed out over the weekend. It's not a big deal.

raw.headline: Oscars? Disney Doesn't Need No Stinkin' Oscar.
raw.summary: The iconic studio collected just one of the 22 Oscars handed out over the weekend. It's not a big deal.

headline: ‘One Battle After Another’ Gets Best Picture as Warner Wins Big
summary: The Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. film, about a washed-up revolutionary seeking to rescue his daughter from his former nemesis, was the favorite among Oscars forecasters and bookmakers going into Sunday’s awards, and it walked away with six wins.  Both frontrunners were from the film division of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. and along with one win for  the studio swept up a total of 11 Oscars, a notable achievement as the company is in the midst of a contentious acquisition by Paramount Skydance Corp.  features memorable character turns by actors including Leonardo DiCaprio, as a pot-smoking anarchist in retirement, and Sean Penn, who plays a white supremacist military officer.

headline: Disney’s Streaming Profit Turn and ESPN NFL Deal Reframe Investment Case
summary: Disney reports that its streaming business has moved past breakeven and is now contributing meaningful operating income. The company introduces "Verts," a short form video experience on Disney+, aimed at boosting digital engagement. ESPN finalizes a wide reaching NFL partnership that includes key distribution rights and an equity stake for the league. Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) is trying to reshape its mix of businesses, with digital now playing a bigger role alongside parks and traditional...

headline: Is The Walt Disney Company (DIS) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
summary: Is DIS a good stock to buy now? We came across a bullish thesis on The Walt Disney Company on Investomine’s Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on DIS. The Walt Disney Company’s share was trading at $101.66 as of March 9th. DIS’s trailing and forward P/E were 14.95 and 15.29 respectively according to […]
